info.description: Functionality to print a document print template on a given printer. Document print templates are managed in Manage Document Print Templates app. Printers are managed in Manage Printers app.
info.title: Print

post.summary: Prints a document print template on a given printer.
post.description: Creates a print task in the status of New. The print task is automatically started. A print task can be in one of the following statuses: New, Started, Failed, or Sent to Printer. You can find the created print task in the Reprint Documents app. A print task consists of sequential calls of data acquisition, document format, and transport plugins. The data acquisition and document format plugins are taken from the document print template. The transport plugin is taken from the printer. By default, a print task is asynchronously executed but it can be run synchronously till its status changes to Sent to Printer.

400.description: The HTTP request is bad or invalid and cannot be processed by the server.

401.description: You are not authorized to call this resource.

403.description: Accessing the resource you are trying to reach is forbidden.

404.description: One of the related elements is not found.

post.summary: Prints a document print template on a given printer. Simplified version.
post.description: Version 2 provides well defined input parameters compared to version 1. It creates a print task with Digital Manufacturing context like plant, resource, order, work center, shop floor control and executes it in the asynchronous or synchronous mode. The task ID is returned. You can find the print task with its actual status in the Reprint Documents app. The printing process consists of data acquisition, document generation, and file transportation. Each step is done by a special plugin. Data acquisition and formatter plugins are assigned to a document print template in the Manage Print Templates app. The transport plugin is defined in the Manage Printers app. During the print task execution its status changes and the Updated Print Task event containing the context is produced. You can create a business rule in the Manage Automatic Triggers app for this event to trace the execution.

post.summary: The service for CPS printing feedbacks.
post.description: This service is used for Cloud Print Service (CPS) to send printing feedbacks to SAP Digital Manufacturing. It is supposed to be called only by CPS. You need to create a destination for this service and use it for the CPS configuration if you want to have an actual print status in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

204.description: The CPS print feedbacks were sucessfully handled.

400.description: The HTTP request is bad or invalid and cannot be processed by the server.

403.description: Accessing the resource you are trying to reach is forbidden.

dataAcquisitionParameters.description:
The JSON object represents parameters for the document data generation. Depending on data acquisition defined in document print template this field can contain:
- For production data acquisition: "plant", "custom_data", "locale", "sfc", or "order".
 Data for the document will be retrieved from this plant, SFC, or order.
- For product genealogy data acquisition: "plant", "locale", "sfc", or "batchNumber".
- For custom data acquisition: "custom_data".
Custom data support objects, arrays, primitives.
Custom data can be bound to a printed document, such as "$.custom_data.custom_field1".

document.description: The document print template to print.

formatParameters.description: The JSON object that represents parameters for the document format plugin. It can have a property "fileName" that contains a file name without an extension for the generated file. By default, the file name is generated from the document print template using the current date and time based on the UTC time zone.

printer.description: The printer to print a document print template.

sync.description: This parameter allows you to run the created print task in the synchronous mode. By default, printing is asynchronous.

PrintRequestV1.description: The request to print a document print template.

numberOfCopies.description: Number of copies to be printed

profile.description: The print queue profile name in the SAP Cloud Print Service. If it's not defined, the Defaults profile will be used.

printTaskId.description: The ID of the created Print Task.

fileName.description: The file name without an extension.

resolution.description:
The resolution for the Adobe Forms ZPL Formatter.

The following default values are supported in the Forms Service by Adobe: 203, 300, and 600. If you don't specify a value, it is 203 by default.

For other resolutions, provide a custom XDC file using the xdcConfig parameter.

xdcConfig.description: The custom XDC configuration file path for the Adobe Forms ZPL Formatter. Use this parameter to specify a custom XDC file instead of the default. The format must be XDC/Customer/.xdc. If it is specified, the resolution parameter is ignored. You can find more details here.

FormatParametersV2.description: The object represents the formatter parameters.

FormatPasParametersV2.description: The object represents the PAS formatter parameters.

document.description: The document print temaplte to print.

documentVersion.description: The version of a document print template to print.

sync.description: The parameter allows you to run printing in the synchronous mode. By default, printing is asynchronous.

customDataAcquisitionParameters.description:
Custom data acquisition parameters.
Use them if the document print template uses custom data acquisition.
You can define your data as JSON to bind it in your XDP template.
 The following JSON types are supported: objects, arrays, and primitives.
Custom data appears under the custom_data element.

PrintRequestV2.description: The request to print a document print template.

ProductGenealogyDataAcquisitionParameter.description: Parameters for Product Genealogy data acquisition.

ProductionDataAcquisitionCustomValueV2.description: The object specifies a custom tag to be returned by Production data acquisition under custom_data element.

customData.description: You can add your data to the data returned by the Production data acquisition to bind it in your XDP template. The custom data appears under the custom_data element.

ProductionDataAcquisitionParametersV2.description: Parameters for the Production data acquisition

status.description: The enumeration represents the CPS printing status.

dateTime.description: The date and time of the feedback in UTC format.

statusDescription.description: The description for a print status.

CpsPrintFeedback.description: The object represents the printing feedback from CPS.

details.description: Contains technical reason for the error.

regionHost.description: The data center where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned for your organization

tokenHost.description: The authentication server where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned for your organization
